title: Indulgent Algerian Bread Tagine
description: A rich and sweet Algerian dish featuring double-rise bread and sweet sherry, made diabetic-friendly with sugar substitutes.
instructions: Cut the double-rise bread into 1-inch cubes and toast until lightly browned.
In a large saucepan, combine the sweet sherry, sugar substitute, orange blossom water, and rose water.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat and simmer for 10 minutes.
Remove the saucepan from the heat and stir in the unsalted butter and almond extract.
Add the toasted bread cubes to the saucepan and toss until the bread is evenly coated with the sherry mixture.
Transfer the bread mixture to a baking dish and bake for 20 minutes.
Remove the dish from the oven and let it cool for 10 minutes.
Dust the top of the tagine with confectioners sugar and serve warm.
